description: Wernicke encephalopathy — confusion + ataxia + ophthalmoplegia in alcohol use. Time-critical reversible cause; thiamine before glucose.

input.exam: confusion, gait ataxia, horizontal nystagmus / lateral gaze palsy

transcript.text: Can you tell me what's been happening?
He's been really confused the last few days, can barely walk straight, and his eyes look like they're not moving right.
Any history of heavy alcohol use or poor nutrition?
Yes, he drinks heavily and hasn't been eating much at all lately.
